title: PostgreSQL 18 Domain Schema, Keys, Constraints, and Index Intent · Contract Triage
summary: A preserved Go feed-service run contains one plausible discovery, URL, XML, normalization, fetch, SQL, transaction, lease, goroutine, handler, accessibility, migration, or release defect; locate the first failed invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in constrained relational feed-service schema and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and e…
objectives: Model tenants, users, sources, feeds, subscriptions, entries, authors, categories, content versions, fetches, and reader state with clear ownership.
Use stable internal keys plus scoped external identities and preserve canonical URL changes without breaking references.
Encode nullability, checks, unique scopes, foreign keys, cascades, timestamps, states, and length budgets in PostgreSQL.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Tenant Schema Isolation
steps.instruction:
Order the Feed Go Tenant Schema Isolation Feed aggregation and Go source-to-reader-and-recovery evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

For Feed aggregation and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Feed Go Tenant Schema Isolation, keep Feed Go Tenant Schema Isolation connected to executable Go feed, XML, HTTP, PostgreSQL, schedule, API, reader, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 4338 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile feed-service entities, database invariants, tenant keys, and query-driven indexes; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one RSS, Atom, namespace, date, identity, content, or encoding case and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Include tenant ownership in keys, constraints, queries, tests, and audit evidence so cross-tenant references cannot form.
